summary: Replace independent shell booleans and browser-global fallbacks with one typed platform runtime composed honestly for web and desktop.

decisions.decision: Require App to receive a ScadMillPlatform value that groups engine, files, dialogs, menus, clipboard, location, persistence, keychain-backed secrets, MCP, window controls, and engine management. Represent optional features with a discriminated available/service union and compose separate web and desktop values at the entry point.
decisions.rationale: Independent booleans and optional services could contradict each other, while clipboard and share-link fallbacks let shared code reach browser globals directly. One typed value makes capability absence explicit, confines globals to adapters, and creates the command/window seams required for the remaining C13 shell work.

testEvidence.criterion: Web and desktop composition declare only implemented capabilities, shared portability/UI code uses injected ports, and existing App behavior remains intact.
testEvidence.test: Exercise both platform factories, the portability controller, source-policy scanner, App recovery/portability journeys, diagnostic clipboard path, and strict TypeScript.

red.outcome: RED: one major unified-contract gap remained; App accepted unrelated platform booleans/services and shared portability/diagnostic code directly accessed browser clipboard globals.

green.command: Focused Vitest platform/portability/source-policy 67/67; focused App/UI 29/29; pnpm.cmd typecheck; node scripts/check-source-policy.mjs; git diff --check
green.outcome: GREEN: all 96 focused tests passed. Strict TypeScript and source policy exited 0; shared UI stayed within the 400-line cap and imports no platform implementation.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
